lede: A look at October 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sophie Thatcher and Michael Dingsdag, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: NASA Astronaut Corps
events.desc: NASA Astronauts Jessica Meir and Christina Koch take part in the first all-female spacewalk when they venture out of the International Space Station to replace a power controller.

events.title: Henrik Igityan National Centre for Aesthetics
events.desc: Based on the world's first children's art museum, the Henrik Igityan National Centre for Aesthetics opened in Yerevan.

events.title: Félicette
events.desc: Félicette, a black and white female Parisian stray cat, becomes the first cat launched into space.

events.title: British Broadcasting Company
events.desc: The British Broadcasting Company (later Corporation) is founded by a consortium, to establish a nationwide network of radio transmitters to provide a national broadcasting service.

events.title: Johannes Brahms
events.desc: Johannes Brahms conducts the premiere of his Double Concerto, composed for violinist Joseph Joachim and cellist Robert Hausmann.

events.title: Siege of Savannah (1779)
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Franco-American Siege of Savannah is lifted.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Burning of Falmouth (now Portland, Maine).

events.title: Hernando de Soto
events.desc: Spanish conquistador Hernando de Soto's forces destroy the fortified town of Mabila in present-day Alabama, killing Tuskaloosa.

events.title: 1356 Basel earthquake
events.desc: Basel earthquake, the most significant historic seismological event north of the Alps, destroys the town of Basel, Switzerland.

events.title: Pappus of Alexandria
events.desc: Pappus of Alexandria, Greek philosopher, observes an eclipse of the Sun and writes a commentary on The Great Astronomer (Almagest).

deaths.text: Bum Phillips, American football player and coach
List of Coronation Street characters introduced in 1962, English actor
Mary of Hungary (governor of the Netherlands), Queen of Hungary and Bohemia from 1516 to 1526

observances.text: Christian feast day: Peter of Alcantara, can also be celebrated on October 19.
Day of Restoration of Independence (Azerbaijan), celebrates the independence of Azerbaijan from the Soviet Union in 1991.
